jokes.text: Oh, my God. It's real. Uh... Yeah, I'm not gonna go deal with this. I'm leaving.
jokes.setup: Tom's disbelief about teenagers having dog poop fights
jokes.punchline: Immediate cowardly retreat upon seeing it's real
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Tom's vanity makes him instantly abandon his duties when faced with actual grossness. Good economy and realistic reaction.

jokes.text: Actually, this is a little fun. This is fun.
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to break up the poop fight
jokes.punchline: She gets caught up and starts enjoying the chaos
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment - her enthusiasm overriding her better judgment. Shows her childlike wonder even in disgusting situations.

jokes.text: That's why I go two towns over if I want to rent a movie with nudity in it.
jokes.setup: Leslie explains why they can't accept gifts due to public scrutiny
jokes.punchline: Overly specific example about her porn-watching habits
jokes.explanation: Excellent Leslie overshare - perfectly captures her earnest rule-following while revealing embarrassing personal details. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Yeah, I probably shouldn't have said that.
jokes.setup: Leslie's overshare about renting porn
jokes.punchline: Immediate self-awareness of her overshare
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective button on the previous joke. Good economy but fairly predictable structure.

jokes.text: You are so pure, you're like a brown Superman with a beard that just stands for justice and truth and the American way.
jokes.setup: Tom claims he's never exploited his government position
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous self-comparison to Superman while literally exploiting his position
jokes.explanation: Great Tom delusion moment - the racial specificity and pompous tone while actively breaking rules creates perfect irony. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Maybe those are his relatives. Yeah, maybe fake boobs run in his family.
jokes.setup: Looking at attractive women on Mark's social media profile
jokes.punchline: Absurd genetic explanation for fake breasts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - treating fake breasts as a hereditary trait. Great economy and unexpected twist on a setup.

jokes.text: She's got a thong on her face.
jokes.setup: Continuing to look at Mark's friend's photos
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact description of ridiculous photo
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about trashy social media photos. Gets the job done but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: His favorite movie is Dances With Wolves.
jokes.setup: Continuing to judge Mark's profile
jokes.punchline: This boring movie choice is treated as equally scandalous
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - treating bad taste in movies as equally shocking as explicit photos. Shows Leslie's priorities.

jokes.text: No matter what direction I move, he's always staring at my chest.
jokes.setup: Leslie talks about feeling intimidated by the wall of male portraits
jokes.punchline: One portrait's eyes seem to follow her chest
jokes.explanation: Classic visual gag with the portrait's eyes following her. Works well with Leslie's paranoia about male-dominated politics.

jokes.text: She swears it makes me look thinner. It kind of does.
jokes.setup: Ron mentions his wife bought him a shirt
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact vanity about looking thinner
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment - Ron's unexpected vanity delivered in his typically deadpan way. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Yes, Ann! That's good, think like a man.
jokes.setup: Ann says she could use a beer
jokes.punchline: Leslie treats this as revolutionary gender-breaking behavior
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment - her over-enthusiasm about breaking gender barriers over something mundane. Shows her earnest feminism.

jokes.text: And it's because you're a woman!
jokes.setup: Leslie tells April she's in charge
jokes.punchline: Making it about gender empowerment unnecessarily
jokes.explanation: Continues Leslie's theme of making everything about gender politics. Good character consistency but not particularly surprising.

jokes.text: That was the sound of a glass ceiling being shattered.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ann arrive at the boys' club
jokes.punchline: Over-dramatic interpretation of joining a casual beer gathering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie delusion - treating a casual workplace social gathering as a historic feminist moment. Good quotability.

jokes.text: I mean, look at those bitches clean up after me.
jokes.setup: Tom claims to already be in the boys' club
jokes.punchline: Casually sexist comment about female coworkers
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's casual misogyny while trying to fit in. Creates discomfort but serves character development.

jokes.text: You have a friend who has a tattoo that says, 'Daddy's Girl.' And both words are misspelled.
jokes.setup: Ann mentions Mark's questionable social media friends
jokes.punchline: The double misspelling makes it even more ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - the misspelling makes the trashy tattoo even more pathetic. Great structure and specificity.

jokes.text: Oh, boo. Brendanawicz, that's some weak sauce. You need to hit harder!
jokes.setup: Mark gives a weak comeback to Ann's roasting
jokes.punchline: Leslie coaches him like it's a sport, disappointed in his performance
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment - treating verbal sparring like a competitive event she's coaching. Shows her competitive nature.

jokes.text: Natalie, it's your sister. Pick me up or I'm gonna draw all over your jeans.
jokes.setup: April makes a phone call
jokes.punchline: Bizarre threat of jean vandalism as leverage
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - the specific weirdness of threatening to draw on jeans is perfectly in character. Highly original and memorable.

jokes.text: 'Cause parties usually get started when you run out of booze.
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to keep the party going after they run out of beer
jokes.punchline: Mark's dry observation about party dynamics
jokes.explanation: Basic sarcasm about party logic. Functional but not particularly creative or character-specific.

jokes.text: I have always tried to live my life in an ethical way, and last night, I failed.
jokes.setup: Leslie's morning confession about opening the gift basket
jokes.punchline: Treating a minor ethics violation like a massive moral failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie over-reaction. Her tendency to catastrophize minor infractions is well-established character trait.

jokes.text: Michele Bachmann, Republican, Minnesota. I am sorry. Tammy Baldwin, Democrat, Wisconsin. I'm so sorry, Tammy.
jokes.setup: Leslie decides to apologize to every female politician alphabetically
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity and personal tone of mass apologies
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leslie escalation - the bureaucratic thoroughness applied to guilt makes it absurdist. Perfect character integration and highly original concept.

jokes.text: She is an angel. And I seriously love her. Hard.
jokes.setup: Andy gushes about Ann being amazing
jokes.punchline: The awkward pause before 'Hard' makes it sound sexual
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment - his innocent but sexually suggestive phrasing. The pause creates the comedy through implication.

jokes.text: I took a bunch of painkillers. They're about to kick in so I'm ready to go.
jokes.setup: Andy is going to clean the house for Ann
jokes.punchline: His solution is recreational drug use
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - treating prescription drugs as cleaning motivation. Dark but fits his irresponsible character perfectly.

jokes.text: It's like I don't even know you anymore.
jokes.setup: Tom acts betrayed by Leslie's rule violation
jokes.punchline: Over-dramatic reaction to someone else breaking rules for once
jokes.explanation: Good Tom hypocrisy - acting scandalized when the rule-follower breaks a rule. Shows his inconsistent morality.

jokes.text: Yes, but she was in the hospital. I did not know that at the time.
jokes.setup: Tom brings up Leslie writing him up for calling his mom
jokes.punchline: Leslie's stubborn defense even when proven wrong
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie stubbornness - even when confronted with reasonable excuse, she doubles down. Shows her inflexible rule-following.

jokes.text: I think you should go to jail. Think about what you've done.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks for April's support
jokes.punchline: April's deadpan suggestion of incarceration
jokes.explanation: Perfect April response - her deadpan cruelty delivered matter-of-factly. Great character integration and timing.

jokes.text: You're a white Protestant man with a full, rich mustache.
jokes.setup: Leslie explains why this isn't a big deal for Mark
jokes.punchline: Including the mustache as a privilege category
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leslie moment - treating facial hair as a form of privilege. Original concept with great economy and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: When you spill something and you try to wipe it up with something that's dirty, then you get double dirty.
jokes.setup: Leslie explains why cover-ups are worse than crimes
jokes.punchline: Childlike metaphor about getting 'double dirty'
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie logic - applying kindergarten wisdom to political scandals. Shows her earnest but simplistic worldview.

jokes.text: I wish I could say some of this mess was Ann's, but it's all mine. I'm kind of a nester.
jokes.setup: Andy surveys his incredibly messy apartment
jokes.punchline: Calling himself a 'nester' while living in squalor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy delusion - reframing his slovenliness as cozy domesticity. Great visual contrast between description and reality.

jokes.text: I'm doing a little experiment tonight to see what will get me drunker. Drinking wine...
jokes.setup: Andy explains his evening plans
jokes.punchline: Treating getting drunk like a scientific experiment
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment - his pseudo-intellectual approach to getting wasted. Fits his lazy but pretentious character.

jokes.text: Because I was waiting for my sister and I was bored, and my hair looked really good.
jokes.setup: April explains why she posted the incriminating video
jokes.punchline: Vanity about her hair as the deciding factor
jokes.explanation: Perfect April logic - causing a government crisis because of a good hair day. Shows her teenage priorities and deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: This bench is so uncomfortable. Ow. Help. Please, Ron. Ron.
jokes.setup: Leslie is having a breakdown about the scandal
jokes.punchline: She's literally uncomfortable on Ron's office bench
jokes.explanation: Minor physical comedy during emotional crisis. Adds texture but not particularly memorable or well-crafted.

jokes.text: Zero to six. I'm gonna write down 10.
jokes.setup: Leslie says she has zero drinks per week
jokes.punchline: Tom arbitrarily inflates the number
jokes.explanation: Good Tom corruption - immediately making Leslie look worse than she is. Shows his untrustworthy nature efficiently.

jokes.text: Have you ever had a sexual dream about our boss, Ron Swanson? No! Absolutely not. No. Yes. No.
jokes.setup: Tom's inappropriate deposition question
jokes.punchline: Leslie's flustered contradiction reveals she has
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - Leslie's contradictory denials reveal the truth. Good structure building to the revelation.

jokes.text: Is he like a regular Ron, or is he half-Ron, half-animal, like a centaur? Is he wearing a football uniform? Are you making love to him on a couch shaped like his mustache? Is he covered in Powerade?
jokes.setup: Tom continues the inappropriate sexual questioning
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre and specific sexual scenarios
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - the mustache couch and Powerade are hilariously specific and unexpected. Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: This is how they clean their dog.
jokes.setup: Andy can't shower with his broken legs
jokes.punchline: He's using a dog-washing method on himself
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy - the dignity of comparing himself to pet grooming. Shows Andy's practical but undignified solutions.

jokes.text: I just put 12 new batteries in that thing!
jokes.setup: Andy is fighting his neighbor over the boom box
jokes.punchline: His priorities are the battery investment, not the theft
jokes.explanation: Good Andy priorities - focusing on the practical loss rather than the principle. Shows his materialistic but petty concerns.

jokes.text: This is not communist China. You cannot make her whip herself. You cannot make her wear a hair shirt. We weren't planning on doing either of those things. This is America. You want to live in North Korea, you can live in North Korea. I don't want to. I want to live in America.
jokes.setup: Ron defends Leslie from the disciplinary committee
jokes.punchline: Increasingly extreme political comparisons to a minor bureaucratic hearing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron escalation - his libertarian paranoia applied to bureaucratic process. Great character consistency and building intensity.

jokes.text: My idea of a perfect government is one guy, who sits in a small room at a desk, and the only thing he's allowed to decide is who to nuke. The man is chosen based on some kind of IQ test, and maybe also a physical tournament, like a decathlon. And women are brought to him, maybe, when he desires them.
jokes.setup: Ron explains he defended Leslie because he hates bureaucracy
jokes.punchline: His ideal government is a dystopian sexual fantasy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron moment - his libertarian ideology taken to its logical extreme becomes fascist dystopia. Highly original, perfectly in character, and darkly funny.

jokes.text: I... We had a brief affair in February of 2004.
jokes.setup: Leslie admits she did everything to impress Mark
jokes.punchline: The overly specific date reveals how much it meant to her
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie vulnerability - the specific date shows how meaningful it was to her. Reveals her romantic desperation effectively.

jokes.text: Smart woman. Iffy choice in men. But then again, I'm not one to talk.
jokes.setup: Ann observes Leslie's situation with Mark
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment about her own dating choices (Andy)
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment from Ann acknowledging her own poor dating choices. Shows character growth and relatability.

jokes.text: I was chasing our neighbor who's a total jagweed and I fell in some prickly bushes.
jokes.setup: Ann asks what happened to Andy's scratches
jokes.punchline: Made-up insult 'jagweed' and childish explanation
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment - the made-up insult and childish story. Shows his immaturity and creative vocabulary.

jokes.text: Somebody is getting gently laid tonight. Andy! God!
jokes.setup: Andy is proud of cleaning the house
jokes.punchline: Crudely announces his sexual expectations, horrifying Ann
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy inappropriateness - the word 'gently' makes it both crude and oddly sweet. Shows his tactless enthusiasm.

jokes.text: I got seven letters in my file. Pretty much every guy in city planning has a bunch of letters.
jokes.setup: Leslie is worried about her letter in her file
jokes.punchline: Mark reveals everyone breaks rules regularly
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that undercuts Leslie's anxiety - shows the boys' club casualness about rule-breaking versus her perfectionism.

jokes.text: Look out, boys. I'm in your club now. I'm in your club. I'm in your club. I'm in your club. I'm in your club. I'm in your... Mmm. Creepy.
jokes.setup: Leslie celebrates being accepted into the boys' club
jokes.punchline: Her repetitive chanting becomes creepy/obsessive
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Leslie's obsessiveness - her triumph becomes unsettling through repetition. Self-aware ending with 'creepy.'
